title: timestamp selection based on now() takes too long
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE sensor_readings (src_ip VARCHAR(15), recv_time TIMESTAMP, emit_time TIMESTAMP, location VARCHAR(30), type VARCHAR(30), value VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO sensor_readings (src_ip, recv_time, emit_time, location, type, value) VALUES ('192.168.1.1', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '10' MINUTE, 'LocationA', 'Type1', 'Value1');
INSERT INTO sensor_readings (src_ip, recv_time, emit_time, location, type, value) VALUES (NULL, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '5' MINUTE, 'LocationB', 'Type2', 'Value2');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sensor_readings WHERE emit_time >= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '1' HOUR;
SELECT MAX(emit_time) FROM sensor_readings WHERE emit_time IS NOT NULL;
